intent: Build a simple Python task management library (tasklib). It is not a production system. Its purpose is to validate that the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline can close a complete dependency chain — from documentation through scaffold to working code — with real merges at each step.
scope_statement:
Build a minimal Python task management library (tasklib) as defined in TRD-TASKLIB, consisting of five sequential dependency layers: documentation (README, ARCHITECTURE, API), package scaffold (src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ subpackages), task model (Task dataclass with TaskStatus enum per FR-1 through FR-7), in-memory task store (InMemoryTaskStore per FR-8 through FR-14), and CLI entry point (add/list/complete commands per FR-15 through FR-19), with a package root re-exporting the public API (FR-20). The purpose is to validate that the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline can close a complete dependency chain — docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI — with real merges at each step, per TRD-TASKLIB §1.

Operator on 'TRD-TASKLIB §6 states no more than 3 implementation files pe':
use the PRD process to resolve any gaps. Use best practices.

Operator on 'FR-1 specifies 'a unique identifier generated automatically ':
use the PRD process to resolve any gaps. Use best practices.

Operator on 'The CLI (FR-15) must be 'runnable as a Python module' — shou':
use the PRD process to resolve any gaps. Use best practices.

Operator on 'Should the test suite be delivered as a separate PR in the d':
use the PRD process to resolve any gaps. Use best practices.

prd_plan.title: Package Scaffold — src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ subpackages
Task Model — Task dataclass and TaskStatus enum (FR-1 through FR-7)
In-Memory Task Store — InMemoryTaskStore (FR-8 through FR-14)
CLI Entry Point — add, list, complete commands (FR-15 through FR-19)
End-to-End Integration Tests and API Documentation Amendment

1.title: Add documentation foundation — README, ARCHITECTURE, API
1.summary: Creates the three seed documentation files that anchor the tasklib dependency chain per PRD-001. README.md describes the library purpose, package structure, and usage example. ARCHITECTURE.md describes the five-layer dependency chain (docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI) with an ASCII diagram. API.md documents all planned public interfaces (Task fields, TaskStatus values, InMemoryTaskStore methods, CLI commands) with signatures, return types, and usage examples. No executable code is included. README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md become immutable after merge; API.md may be amended by a future update PR when interfaces are implemented.

2.title: Add tasklib package scaffold
2.summary: Create the minimal src/tasklib package skeleton with models and storage subpackages and empty __init__.py module entry points, establishing the import structure without any business logic.

3.title: Implement TaskStatus enum and Task dataclass
3.summary: Add the TaskStatus enum and Task dataclass implementing the documented task model requirements from API.md, including automatic uuid4 identifier generation, created_at timestamp, and core validation-friendly behavior. Re-exports Task and TaskStatus from the models subpackage __init__.py.

4.title: Re-export task model types from package root
4.summary: Expose Task and TaskStatus through the tasklib package public API so downstream layers can import from tasklib directly, in line with the planned interface contract documented in API.md.

5.title: Implement InMemoryTaskStore
5.summary: Add InMemoryTaskStore with CRUD-style task management operations (add, get, list, complete, delete) required by the storage layer, using the Task model as its persisted in-memory entity. Re-exports InMemoryTaskStore from the storage subpackage __init__.py.

6.title: Re-export InMemoryTaskStore from package root
6.summary: Expose InMemoryTaskStore through the tasklib package root public API to complete the library-facing import surface described in API.md.

7.title: Add tasklib CLI module
7.summary: Implement a module-runnable CLI entry point supporting add, list, and complete commands using argparse. Includes __main__.py delegating to cli.py so the interface is runnable via python -m tasklib.

8.title: Amend API.md to match implemented interfaces
8.summary: Update API.md after all implementation PRs have merged to confirm final public signatures, CLI invocation details, return types, and usage examples based on the actual merged code. This is an amendment to the existing file, not a re-creation.
